source_issue.title: News portlet does not use effective/expiration dates

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses a bug in the News portlet of Plone where it displays items even if they are outside their set effective and expiration dates. The intention is to enhance the functionality by respecting these dates.

## What needs to be done
We need to update the News portlet so that it only displays items that are currently effective. Specifically, it should not show items that are either before their effective date or after their expiration date. There is additional mention that this issue also affects the Events portlet.

## Helpful background
Effective and expiration dates are settings for content items that control when they should appear or disappear in listings. This issue is confirmed and categorized as a bug, so resolving it will improve user experience by ensuring the correct items are shown in the portlet.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the code for the News portlet in the [plone/plone.app.portlets](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.portlets) repository.
2. Check the current implementation of effective and expiration date functions.
3. Look into any existing tests for the News portlet, or consider writing new ones to ensure proper functionality.
4. Coordinate with other contributors if you have questions, especially about related issues with the Events portlet.
